name: Joash and the repair of the temple
notes: Kings and Chronicles tell the same deliverance: Athaliah destroys the seed royal, the infant Joash is stolen away and hidden in the house of the LORD six years, and in the seventh year Jehoiada the priest makes a covenant with the captains, brings forth the king's son with the crown and the testimony, and the people cry 'God save the king'; Athaliah cries 'Treason, Treason' and is slain, the house of Baal is broken down, and Joash later orders the temple repaired by the workmen. The shared core runs almost word for word through the crowning, the slaying of Athaliah, and the gathering of money into a chest for the breaches of the house. Each account keeps its own vantage: Kings details the threefold division of the guard on the sabbath, the chest set beside the altar, and that no vessels of silver or gold were made; Chronicles, written from the temple's side, gives the Levites' priestly role, names the captains, and tells that the sons of Athaliah had broken up the house of God. These are complementary vantages on one event and are left to read fully in place.
